content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

May 25, 1976

Mr. Richard Schmechel
California Labor Federation
995 Market Street, Suite 312
San Francisco, California 94103

Dear Mr. Schmechel:

This letter is to confirm our May 21 and 24, 1976 telephone conversations regarding campaign disclosure requirements of the Political Reform Act.

Your question concerned a matter about which I had given advice to Bruce Adams in my letter to him of May 12, 1976, a copy of which is enclosed. You stated the following facts: On the date the expenditures in connection with the mailing to the Federation's members were made, no one with authority to sign checks in behalf of the Federation was available. Consequently, the Federation received on the same date from COPE a loan to cover the cost of the mailing. You asked whether the Federation, as a result of receiving funds, would become a recipient committee under Government Code Section 82013(a).

I informed you that the Federation would be required to file campaign statements on June 1, 1976 and no later than August 17, 1976, as either a recipient committee or a major donor committee as follows: (1) If the Federation received funds from COPE to make the expenditures, the Federation would become a recipient committee required to register with the Secretary of State and to file campaign statements reporting the expenditures in connection with the mailing and receipt of the loan from COPE. (2) If, on the other hand, COPE reported the expenditures on its campaign statement, the Federation could by making a contribution to COPE sufficient to cover COPE's costs, become a major donor committee under Government Code Section 82013(c) and thereby be required to file campaign statements reporting only the contribution to COPE.

Please contact our staff if we can be of further assistance.

Sincerely,

NAN HAMBLETON
Special Compliance Representative

NH:mak
bcc: Jeanne [signature]
Enclosure

sections.question: Whether the California Labor Federation, as a result of receiving funds from COPE to cover the cost of a mailing to the Federation's members, would become a recipient committee under Government Code Section 82013(a).
sections.conclusion: The Federation would be required to file campaign statements on June 1, 1976 and no later than August 17, 1976, as either a recipient committee or a major donor committee as follows: (1) If the Federation received funds from COPE to make the expenditures, the Federation would become a recipient committee required to register with the Secretary of State and to file campaign statements reporting the expenditures in connection with the mailing and receipt of the loan from COPE. (2) If COPE reported the expenditures on its campaign statement, the Federation could by making a contribution to COPE sufficient to cover COPE's costs, become a major donor committee under Government Code Section 82013(c) and thereby be required to file campaign statements reporting only the contribution to COPE.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether the California Labor Federation becomes a recipient committee under Government Code Section 82013(a) when it receives a loan from COPE to cover campaign expenditures for a mailing to its members.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The California Labor Federation becomes a recipient committee if it receives funds from COPE to make the expenditures and must file campaign statements accordingly. Alternatively, if COPE reports the expenditures, the Federation could become a major donor committee by contributing to COPE to cover COPE's costs.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Letter confirms telephone conversations rather than responding to a written request. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION section headers present, but content clearly addresses specific legal question and provides explicit advice. Minor OCR artifacts present in document text.

QUESTION: Whether the California Labor Federation, as a result of receiving funds from COPE to cover the cost of a mailing to the Federation's members, would become a recipient committee under Government Code Section 82013(a).

CONCLUSION: The Federation would be required to file campaign statements on June 1, 1976 and no later than August 17, 1976, as either a recipient committee or a major donor committee as follows: (1) If the Federation received funds from COPE to make the expenditures, the Federation would become a recipient committee required to register with the Secretary of State and to file campaign statements reporting the expenditures in connection with the mailing and receipt of the loan from COPE. (2) If COPE reported the expenditures on its campaign statement, the Federation could by making a contribution to COPE sufficient to cover COPE's costs, become a major donor committee under Government Code Section 82013(c) and thereby be required to file campaign statements reporting only the contribution to COPE.

embedding.summary: FPPC advises the California Labor Federation on campaign disclosure requirements under the Political Reform Act regarding a loan received from COPE to cover mailing expenditures. The Federation must file as either a recipient committee or major donor committee depending on whether it received the funds directly or indirectly through COPE.
